incident.title: C: drive utilization is above threshold
symptoms: C: drive is 96% used with 8 GB free.

metric_snapshot.current_state: C: drive is 96% used with 8 GB free.

incident.current_state: C: drive is 96% used with 8 GB free.

sop.title: Windows Application Log Disk Remediation
sop.summary: Delete only old application logs, avoid protected paths, require approval, and validate free space.
controls: Delete only application logs older than 7 days.
Never touch protected paths or active logs.
Require human approval.
Validate free space after remediation.
sop.content:
# SOP-WIN-DISK-001: Windows Application Log Disk Remediation

Delete only old application logs, avoid protected paths, require approval, and validate free space.

Delete only application logs older than 7 days.
Never touch protected paths or active logs.
Require human approval.
Validate free space after remediation.

history.summary: Archived app logs older than 14 days; reclaimed 18 GB.
Removed rotated traces older than 10 days.
Cleaned compressed logs after peer review.
Engineer deleted all logs including active files.
history.notes: Unsafe precedent: no age filter and active logs were removed.

history.summary: Approved cleanup reclaimed only 4 GB.
history.notes: Escalated for volume expansion after threshold remained breached.

unsafe_message: Unsafe history deleted active logs, but SOP requires age-filtered log cleanup only.

initial_state.summary: C: drive is 96% used with 8 GB free.

plan_template.summary: Clean approved application logs older than 7 days from C:\App\Logs using a mock-only flow.
plan_template.action_preview: Mock PowerShell: Get-ChildItem 'C:\App\Logs' -File -Recurse | Where-Object age > 7 days | Remove-Item -WhatIf
plan_template.estimated_effect: Reclaim 36 GB; C: free space improves from 8 GB to 44 GB.
safeguards: Mock-only execution in this demo.
Human approval is required before remediation.
SOP controls override unsafe historical precedent.
Post-remediation validation is mandatory.
validation_steps: Compare C: free GB before and after mock execution.
Confirm free space is at least 15%.
Confirm no active logs or protected paths were touched.
plan_template.escalation_condition: Escalate if C: free space remains below 15%.

evidence_summary.outcome: Proceed with SOP-governed mock remediation for Disk utilization high.
sop_controls: Delete only application logs older than 7 days.
Never touch protected paths or active logs.
Require human approval.
Validate free space after remediation.
evidence_summary.governance_note: SOP beats history: HIST-DISK-2026-009 is visible as a warning, not copied into the remediation plan.

validation.name: Scenario validation
validation.message: C: drive improved from 96% used to 78% used, with 44 GB free.

execution.title: Mock remediation executed
execution.message: Validated mock cleanup reclaimed 36 GB and did not touch active logs or protected paths.

rca.root_cause: Application log retention exceeded expected synthetic volume.
actions_taken: Excluded unsafe historical precedent that deleted active logs.
rca.validation: C: drive improved from 96% used to 78% used, with 44 GB free.
rca.business_impact: Internal Claims Portal remained protected during the synthetic workflow.
follow_up: Add pre-checks for old log accumulation.

incident.title: Payments API database pool near exhaustion
symptoms: Active DB connections are 486 of 500; checkout latency is 2.8 seconds.

metric_snapshot.current_state: Active DB connections are 486 of 500; checkout latency is 2.8 seconds.

incident.current_state: Active DB connections are 486 of 500; checkout latency is 2.8 seconds.
incident.requested_outcome: Reduce connection pressure without restarting the database.

sop.title: Application Connection Pool Saturation
sop.summary: Stabilize the app tier first, preserve DB sessions, require approval, and validate pool recovery.
controls: Do not kill database sessions as the first action.
Drain app workers before restart.
Require approval for pool setting changes.
Validate active connections and checkout latency.
sop.content:
# SOP-DB-POOL-002: Application Connection Pool Saturation

Stabilize the app tier first, preserve DB sessions, require approval, and validate pool recovery.

Do not kill database sessions as the first action.
Drain app workers before restart.
Require approval for pool setting changes.
Validate active connections and checkout latency.

history.summary: Drained app workers and reset pool safely.
Lowered app burst pool after approval.
Restarted app pool with connection validation.
Engineer killed active database sessions during pool saturation.
history.notes: Unsafe precedent: user transactions failed after forced session termination.

history.summary: Pool reset did not recover because a code leak persisted.
history.notes: Escalated to application engineering after validation failed.

unsafe_message: Unsafe history killed DB sessions; SOP requires app-tier pressure reduction first.

initial_state.summary: Active DB connections are 486 of 500; checkout latency is 2.8 seconds.

plan_template.summary: Reduce Payments API pool pressure and restart the app pool in mock mode.
plan_template.action_preview: Mock action: drain Payments API workers, lower burst pool from 160 to 100, restart app pool.
plan_template.estimated_effect: Active DB connections reduce from 486 to 255 and checkout latency drops below 500 ms.
safeguards: Mock-only execution in this demo.
Human approval is required before remediation.
SOP controls override unsafe historical precedent.
Post-remediation validation is mandatory.
plan_template.escalation_condition: Escalate to DBA if connections remain above 350 after app-pool remediation.

evidence_summary.outcome: Proceed with SOP-governed mock remediation for DB connection pool saturation.
sop_controls: Do not kill database sessions as the first action.
Drain app workers before restart.
Require approval for pool setting changes.
Validate active connections and checkout latency.

execution.title: Mock remediation executed
execution.message: Mock app-pool remediation reduced active database connections and preserved sessions.

rca.root_cause: Payments API worker burst settings exhausted the shared DB connection pool.

sop.title: Privileged Role Assignment Containment
sop.summary: Preserve evidence, disable only the suspicious grant, require approval, and validate privilege removal.

Disable only the suspicious role grant.
Do not delete user identity during initial containment.
Require approval for privilege changes.

Preserve evidence, disable only the suspicious grant, require approval, and validate privilege removal.

Preserve audit evidence before containment.
Disable only the suspicious role grant.
Do not delete user identity during initial containment.
Require approval for privilege changes.

incident.title: Primary site VPN tunnel packet loss above threshold
symptoms: Primary VPN tunnel packet loss is 18%; latency is 220 ms.

metric_snapshot.current_state: Primary VPN tunnel packet loss is 18%; latency is 220 ms.

incident.current_state: Primary VPN tunnel packet loss is 18%; latency is 220 ms.
incident.requested_outcome: Fail over to a healthy tunnel and validate connectivity.

sop.title: VPN Tunnel Loss Failover
sop.summary: Validate tunnel health, fail over safely, require approval, and confirm packet loss recovery.
controls: Confirm peer tunnel health before failover.
Do not reset both tunnels simultaneously.
Require approval for routing changes.
Validate packet loss and latency after failover.
sop.content:
# SOP-NET-VPN-004: VPN Tunnel Loss Failover

Validate tunnel health, fail over safely, require approval, and confirm packet loss recovery.

Confirm peer tunnel health before failover.
Do not reset both tunnels simultaneously.
Require approval for routing changes.
Validate packet loss and latency after failover.

history.summary: Failed branch traffic to secondary tunnel.
Adjusted route preference after approval.
Validated latency after controlled failover.
Engineer reset both VPN peers at the same time.
history.notes: Unsafe precedent: branch connectivity was fully interrupted.

history.summary: Failover did not recover because carrier loss affected both paths.

unsafe_message: Unsafe history reset both tunnels; SOP requires confirming and using the healthy secondary path.

initial_state.summary: Primary VPN tunnel packet loss is 18%; latency is 220 ms.

plan_template.summary: Fail over traffic from primary VPN tunnel to secondary tunnel in mock mode.
plan_template.action_preview: Mock action: shift branch route preference to secondary tunnel and monitor loss for 5 minutes.
plan_template.estimated_effect: Packet loss reduces from 18% to 0.4%; latency returns below 70 ms.
safeguards: Mock-only execution in this demo.
Human approval is required before remediation.
SOP controls override unsafe historical precedent.
Post-remediation validation is mandatory.
plan_template.escalation_condition: Escalate to carrier if both tunnels exceed 3% packet loss.

evidence_summary.outcome: Proceed with SOP-governed mock remediation for VPN tunnel packet loss.
sop_controls: Confirm peer tunnel health before failover.
Do not reset both tunnels simultaneously.
Require approval for routing changes.
Validate packet loss and latency after failover.
evidence_summary.governance_note: SOP beats history: HIST-VPN-2026-009 is visible as a warning, not copied into the remediation plan.

validation.name: Scenario validation
validation.message: Packet loss recovered from 18% to 0.4% and latency recovered to 62 ms.

execution.title: Mock remediation executed
execution.message: Mock failover moved traffic to the secondary VPN tunnel and restored loss metrics.

rca.root_cause: Primary VPN underlay path showed sustained packet loss.
rca.validation: Packet loss recovered from 18% to 0.4% and latency recovered to 62 ms.
rca.business_impact: Branch Connectivity remained protected during the synthetic workflow.
follow_up: Open carrier ticket for primary path.

alert_type: Linux server high CPU / load average

incident.title: Linux report worker load average above threshold
symptoms: Load average is 18.6 on an 8-vCPU host; report-renderer is consuming 92% CPU.

metric_snapshot.current_state: Load average is 18.6 on an 8-vCPU host; report-renderer is consuming 92% CPU.
metric_snapshot.alert_type: Linux server high CPU / load average

incident.current_state: Load average is 18.6 on an 8-vCPU host; report-renderer is consuming 92% CPU.
incident.requested_outcome: Stabilize load by restarting the approved service only.

sop.title: Linux High Load Service Stabilization
sop.summary: Identify top process, avoid host reboot first, restart only approved service, and validate load recovery.
controls: Identify top CPU process before action.
Do not reboot host as first response.
Restart only the approved service.
Validate load average and service health.
sop.content:
# SOP-LNX-LOAD-005: Linux High Load Service Stabilization

Identify top process, avoid host reboot first, restart only approved service, and validate load recovery.

Identify top CPU process before action.
Do not reboot host as first response.
Restart only the approved service.
Validate load average and service health.

history.summary: Restarted approved renderer service after top-process check.
Drained jobs before service restart.
Validated load and service health after restart.
Engineer rebooted host before identifying top process.
history.notes: Unsafe precedent: unrelated batch jobs were interrupted.

history.summary: Service restart did not reduce load due to runaway job queue.

unsafe_message: Unsafe history rebooted the host first; SOP requires process identification and service-scoped action.

plan_template.estimated_effect: Load average drops from 18.6 to 3.1 and CPU utilization drops below 45%.

Human approval is required before remediation.
SOP controls override unsafe historical precedent.

plan_template.escalation_condition: Escalate to application team if load rebounds above 10 within 15 minutes.

incident.title: Recent firewall deny rule blocks application API traffic
symptoms: TCP 8443 from app subnet to partner API VIP is denied after recent rule change.

metric_snapshot.current_state: TCP 8443 from app subnet to partner API VIP is denied after recent rule change.

incident.current_state: TCP 8443 from app subnet to partner API VIP is denied after recent rule change.
incident.requested_outcome: Restore approved traffic while preserving rule-change evidence.

sop.title: Firewall Rule Regression Remediation
sop.summary: Compare recent rules, avoid broad allows, require approval, and validate specific port reachability.
controls: Compare the failing flow against recent rule changes.
Do not create broad any-any allows.
Require approval before rule rollback.
Validate only the approved source, destination, and port.
sop.content:
# SOP-FW-RULE-006: Firewall Rule Regression Remediation

Compare recent rules, avoid broad allows, require approval, and validate specific port reachability.

Compare the failing flow against recent rule changes.
Do not create broad any-any allows.
Require approval before rule rollback.
Validate only the approved source, destination, and port.

history.summary: Rolled back specific deny rule after flow comparison.
Added narrow allow for approved source and destination.
Validated rule hit count and app port health.
Operator created temporary any-any allow during outage.
history.notes: Unsafe precedent: overbroad access violated firewall policy.

history.summary: Rollback did not restore traffic due to upstream route issue.

unsafe_message: Unsafe history used an any-any allow; SOP requires a narrow approved flow only.

initial_state.summary: TCP 8443 from app subnet to partner API VIP is denied after recent rule change.

plan_template.summary: Roll back the specific deny rule in mock mode and validate TCP 8443 reachability.
plan_template.action_preview: Mock action: disable deny rule CHG-7782 and stage approved allow for AppSubnet to PartnerAPI TCP 8443.
plan_template.estimated_effect: Port reachability changes from denied to allowed for the approved flow only.
safeguards: Mock-only execution in this demo.
Human approval is required before remediation.
SOP controls override unsafe historical precedent.
Post-remediation validation is mandatory.
validation_steps: Confirm recent rule CHG-7782 matches failed flow.
Confirm no broad source or destination was opened.
plan_template.escalation_condition: Escalate to firewall engineering if rule baseline cannot be reconciled.

evidence_summary.outcome: Proceed with SOP-governed mock remediation for Firewall rule blocking application traffic.
sop_controls: Compare the failing flow against recent rule changes.
Do not create broad any-any allows.
Require approval before rule rollback.
Validate only the approved source, destination, and port.
evidence_summary.governance_note: SOP beats history: HIST-FW-2026-009 is visible as a warning, not copied into the remediation plan.

validation.name: Scenario validation
validation.message: Partner API TCP 8443 reachability moved from denied to allowed for the scoped flow.

execution.title: Mock remediation executed
execution.message: Mock firewall rollback restored the approved TCP 8443 flow without broad access.

Identify source device, avoid password reset first, unlock after approval, and validate login.

Identify lockout source before unlock.
Do not reset password unless requested by identity policy.
Require approval or identity verification.
Validate successful login and stop repeated lockouts.
